content.claim: KLIK centralization was justified on cost-equalization grounds but has produced documented budget failures: KLIK has been in deficit since its first year despite receiving billions of forints annually, and previously better-off urban schools experienced substantial funding declines.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Underfunding may reflect systemic budget constraints rather than centralization design failure
Does not isolate whether closures would improve fiscal outcomes
No counterfactual analysis of municipal-run budgets under pre-2013 system
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
